title: Blotterbook — improvement backlog (paste-ready prompts)
intro: From the 2026-06-25 audit. Each item is a self-contained prompt: scope, goal, constraints, "done when".
tags: Tags: P1 do-soon · P2 durable win · P3 polish. Send them one at a time (one surface/concern per turn).
note: Committed source of truth for the ACTIVE engineering backlog (open + guardrail items; replaces the retired .claude/backlog.md). Completed (Done) items are archived in data/backlog_archive.json to keep this file lean — the admin Backlog panel reads BOTH and merges them, so done items stay visible. The admin panel shows category/effort/status only; prompt + doneNote stay in the JSON.
commitConvention: Conventional-commit titles (enables CH12 automated versioning): when committing, and ESPECIALLY when titling a PR / squash-merge, lead the title with a conventional-commit type so the version bump can be derived automatically — `feat:` (new capability → minor), `fix:` (bug fix → patch), `chore:`/`refactor:`/`docs:`/`style:`/`test:`/`perf:` (→ patch), and append `!` (e.g. `feat!:`) or a `BREAKING CHANGE:` footer for a breaking change (→ major). Scope it when useful, e.g. `feat(staging): …`. Untyped/legacy titles default to a patch bump. Keep the existing Co-Authored-By and Claude-Session trailers on the commit body.
donePolicy: When an item's status is set to `done`, delete its `prompt` field and keep only the `doneNote` — the doneNote is the shipped record of what was actually done, so the original prompt is redundant and only bloats this file (the admin backlog panel never renders done-item prompts anyway). Keep `prompt` on `open` and `guardrail` items, since those are still the actionable spec. Applies retroactively: a done item should never carry a prompt. RECURRING ITEMS: an item with `recurring: true` (e.g. R1 repo audit, CH16 staging→prod promotion) is a perpetual driver — NEVER set its status to done, set completedDate, or strip its prompt; it stays open across every run and its prompt leads with a RECURRING/REPEATABLE marker. Each run ships its output as new items, not by closing the recurring item. ARCHIVE (post-split): completed items now live in `data/backlog_archive.json`. When you set an item to `done` and strip its prompt, MOVE the item object out of backlog.json into backlog_archive.json (the admin panel merges both). Recurring items never move — they stay open in backlog.json.

items.title: Keep the Store interface as the only persistence seam
items.prompt: Guardrail for future work: all reads/writes stay behind `Store` (`app/store.js`) so the planned cloud/ sync tier can drop in a `CloudStore` with the same async methods. Reject any change that calls `indexedDB` directly from app/render code.

items.title: Second full repo audit
items.prompt: RECURRING — leave this item OPEN; never mark it done, set completedDate, or strip this prompt. Each run is a fresh full audit (this is the Nth, not a one-shot); the findings ship as NEW backlog items, and R1 itself stays open for the next pass. Read-only audit (NO code edits) of the current repo: architecture, code quality, duplicated code, and security posture — accounting for the CURRENT architecture (ADR-001): a Vite build to `dist/` (A26) and a Svelte 5 SPA on ALL app surfaces after the A33 cutover (app/demo/staging mount `app/staging-svelte/main.js`; the vanilla view layer was deleted; the pure-logic core — adapters/compute+costModel in core.js/store/demostore/curveseries/report/sampledata/util — is reused verbatim per A29), the mode-aware Store seam via context('bb:store'), `style-src 'self'` CSP (S18/A55, no inline styles), and two-track automated versioning (CH12). Give concrete, prioritized findings, and append any new actions to this backlog in the same format. Done when: a written, prioritized audit is delivered and new follow-ups are added to the backlog.

items.title: Guardrail: verify Stripe webhook signature before provisioning
items.prompt: Guardrail for the payments scaffold: never provision an account/entitlement in `functions/api/webhook.js` before verifying the Stripe signature — HMAC-SHA256 over the RAW request body with `STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET`, constant-time compare, and a timestamp/replay window. The endpoint is an inert 501 stub today; reject any future change that wires up provisioning without signature verification first.

items.title: Promote Staging to Prod
items.prompt:
REPEATABLE — leave this item OPEN; never mark it done. Run to promote staging-only feature(s) onto prod + demo. Canonical procedure: the README section 'Promoting a feature from staging → prod' (the gate-layer model + 8-step checklist) — read it first; this prompt is the operational driver, the README is the source of truth.

SCOPE: promote the feature(s) named in the request. If none are named, inventory the full staging delta and promote all of it (staging is a proving ground — every feature is destined for prod + demo). Build the inventory by grepping: `STAGING_PAGE` and `if(!STAGING_PAGE)` / `if(STAGING_PAGE)` guards in the `app/*.js` ES modules (there is no longer a separate `app/staging.js` — it was renamed to `app/widgets.js` and loaded on every surface; staging-only logic now lives behind `STAGING_PAGE` guards inside the shared modules); `<!--IF mode=staging-->` blocks in `partials/*.html`; inline markup in `app/staging.html` that isn't yet in a partial; and the `STAGING_ONLY` set in `scripts/bump-version.mjs`. List each feature and its gate layers before editing.

PER FEATURE: (1) un-gate JS — delete the `STAGING_PAGE` runtime guard so it runs on every surface, or move staging-only logic into the relevant shared `app/*.js` module. (A20: the app is native ES modules loaded via a single `<script type=module src=main.js>` entry — if you add a NEW module file, `import` it from `app/main.js` rather than adding a `<script>` to `partials/app-scripts.html`.) (2) un-gate HTML — move inline staging markup into the right `partials/app-*.html`, or widen its `<!--IF mode=staging-->` to the modes it should reach (normally all three). (3) DEMO 1:1 — the feature MUST appear on demo, single-sourced from the SAME partial (never fork a separate demo copy — that is exactly what drifted before), with every TRADE-mutating control `disabled` in the `<!--IF mode=demo-->` path AND guarded by `DEMO_MODE`; read-only features (activity terminal, session pill) and UI-preference state (saved layouts) just mirror over. (4) if a path is no longer staging-exclusive, update the `STAGING_ONLY` set in `scripts/bump-version.mjs`.

THEN: run `node scripts/build-includes.mjs`; verify all three surfaces in a browser (prod: feature works and persists; demo: feature visible with trade-mutations disabled and nothing saved; staging: unchanged); run the gate — `node scripts/test-adapters.mjs`, `test-version.mjs`, `test-auth.mjs`, `test-flags.mjs`, `test-tax.mjs`, plus build-includes/build-manifest idempotency. Title the commit/PR `feat:` (or `fix:`) so CH12 bumps prod by the right level automatically — do NOT hand-edit `data/versions.json`; staging keeps its lead. Add a `data/changelog.json` (F13) entry for the new prod version describing what shipped. Commit + push (open a PR only if asked).

Each run done when: the in-scope staging features are live on prod + demo at 1:1 (demo trade-mutations disabled), all three surfaces verified, the gate is green, a changelog entry is added, and the change is committed/pushed. Leave this item OPEN (repeatable).

items.title: Discussion & planning: Trade Replay (simulated market snapshots)
items.prompt: Scope the effort + requirements for a Trade Replay feature that lets users step through simulated market snapshots in time around a trade to review it for analysis. Cover: what data is needed (do current platform exports carry intra-trade price/time, or only fills? what extra market data would replay require and from where?), storage/perf implications for a client-side static-hosted app, the UI surface, and a phased build estimate. Done when: a written feasibility/effort estimate exists with concrete next-step items and open questions.

items.title: Discussion & planning: module menu (spawn modules onto the dashboard)
items.prompt: Scope a Quantower-inspired 'module menu': a menu listing all of the app's modules where the user clicks an item to spawn that module onto their dashboard. Assess fit with the current panel/widget system (data-key panels, drag-reorder/collapse, layout templates via ws_save), what spawnable/removable modules require vs today's fixed panel set, how user-composed layouts persist, and a phased estimate. Done when: a written effort estimate + requirements + next-step items exist.

items.title: Discussion & planning: realtime resizable + snapping modules
items.prompt: Scope realtime resizable modules with Quantower-style snapping (windows/modules freely resize and snap/dock together). Assess against the current drag-reorder/collapse layout system and the CSS layout it uses, what a resize+snap engine needs (library vs custom), mobile implications, layout persistence, and perf. Done when: a written effort estimate + requirements + next-step items + open questions exist.

items.title: Discussion & planning: economic calendar data on the calendar module
items.prompt: Scope adding economic-event data (FOMC and other standard futures-relevant events) to the calendar module, user-togglable, with ~5 years of historical events backed in. Assess: data sourcing/licensing, how/where the dataset is stored and shipped (static JSON? payload size for 5 years?), how events render on the calendar without crowding the existing trade/journal data, the toggle UX, and ongoing updates/maintenance. Done when: a written effort estimate + requirements + next-step items + open questions exist.

items.title: Discussion & planning: Stripe integration (donations now, subscriptions later)
items.prompt: Scope the effort + requirements to set up Stripe. NEAR-TERM goal: accept donations — the user clicks/interacts with the pricing module on the homepage to trigger a Stripe donation flow. Cover: Stripe primitives for one-off donations (Payment Links vs Checkout Session vs Payment Element), what server side is needed on Cloudflare Pages (a Pages Function for the Checkout session + webhook, secret/key handling — no keys in the static client), the homepage UX hook on the existing pricing element, and test vs live mode. ALSO lay down scaffolding for monthly SUBSCRIPTIONS (Products/Prices, customer + billing portal, webhooks) even though subscriptions are a later goal gated on remote data storage + user accounts — note the dependency and what can be stubbed now vs deferred. Done when: a written effort estimate + requirements (donations MVP path, subscription scaffolding, server/secret needs, open questions) + concrete next-step backlog items exist.

items.title: Add a copyright + operator line to the site footer
items.prompt: From R7. The shared site footer (partials/footer.html) has no copyright line and never names the operating entity. Add a '© 2026 <name>' line (and, on legal.html, a 'Blotterbook is operated by <entity>' note). The exact entity display name is a pending owner decision (TBD as of R7 — 'Scaline Audio' per LICENSE); ship brand-only '© 2026 Blotterbook' now if undecided and swap in the entity once chosen. Rebuild includes so every page picks up the footer change. Done when: every page footer shows a copyright line and the operator is named once the entity is decided.

items.title: Pre-monetization legal pack: ToS, Privacy Policy, Donations/Refund policy
items.prompt: From R7 — prerequisite for R15 (Stripe). Before taking money, expand the legal content (legal.html / new sections or pages): (1) a real Terms of Service distinct from the current disclaimer bullets, with a governing-law/venue clause set to DELAWARE; (2) a standalone full Privacy Policy (today it's a summary) covering local-only storage, the coarse-region tax lookup, and any Stripe data once payments land; (3) a Donations/Refund policy stating the $25/$50 tiers are voluntary NON-REFUNDABLE donations with no goods/services exchanged; (4) name the operating entity / seller identity (BLOCKED on the owner's entity decision from R7). Keep the existing not-a-broker/not-advice disclaimers. Link the new docs from the footer and from the donation/checkout flow. Done when: ToS (Delaware), Privacy Policy, and the non-refundable Donations/Refund policy are published and linked, and the seller identity is shown.

items.title: Clear stale "(staging)" labels after the F14 promotion + dead degraded-state branch
items.prompt: From the R1 audit (cosmetic drift from the CH16 F14 promotion). F14's stat-card detail modal now ships on all surfaces, but three comment/labels still call it staging-only: app/widgets.js ~116 ('F14 (staging) — stat-card metric-detail modals'), app/app.css ~174 ('clickable headline cards (staging)'), and app/app.css ~388 ('stat-card detail modal (staging)'). Update them to 'all surfaces · CH16' to match render.js/widgets.js/partials, so a future reader doesn't think the feature is still gated. Also: setSession('degraded') (app/widgets.js ~29-33) handles a state no caller emits (self-labeled 'reserved/forward-looking') — either wire it or drop the dead branch. Done when: no stale '(staging)' label remains for F14 and the degraded branch is wired or removed. R1 FOLLOW-UP (additional stale post-CH16 '(staging)' labels found in this audit, clear these too): app/export.js ~186 still heads the export-report modal feature 'F3 (staging)' though export.js ~177 says it was promoted to all surfaces in CH16; and scripts/build-includes.mjs ~24-26 header comment still claims the activity terminal is staging-ALONE and NOT duplicated, but CH16 promoted it to all three shells (now identical in app/demo/staging.html).

items.title: (homepage) Add topic graphics to the feature-detail panels
items.prompt: From F7's feature explorer. The homepage Features section (index.html + assets/home.js) shows a clickable feature LIST that swaps a detail panel on selection; those detail panels are currently text-only. Add a topic graphic to each feature's detail panel that visually represents the feature — reuse/adapt the app's OWN module visuals where possible (e.g. a mini equity curve for the performance feature, a month grid for the calendar/journal, a cost waterfall for the cost/tax feature, a stat-card cluster for statistics). Keep it dependency-free (inline SVG, colours from tokens.css), responsive (the explorer stacks to one column at md/760), and CSP-clean (no inline scripts/handlers — wire via assets/home.js). Done when: each feature-detail panel includes a relevant graphic, the panels still swap on selection, and the section renders responsively with zero console/CSP errors.

items.title: (staging) Review: purpose of the 'Tagged trades' Overview stat + what tagging means
items.prompt: Staging-scoped discussion. Review the 'Tagged trades' stat in the Manage data → Overview section: where its value comes from today (the count of trades in the trademeta store carrying tags), what tagging a trade currently MEANS in the app (per-trade tags set in the trade editor, surfaced in the dashboard Tag filter), and what it SHOULD mean as a workflow (setups? mistakes? strategies? review state?). Decide whether the Overview stat earns its place, how it should be defined/labeled, and whether tagging needs a clearer purpose or UX. Any resulting UI change is staging-only (proving-ground) until promoted via CH16. Done when: a written decision on the stat's meaning + tagging's intended purpose exists, with concrete next-step items if changes are warranted.

items.title: (staging) Make the 'Erase all local data' button red (danger styling)
items.prompt: Staging-only — gate to the staging surface; do NOT change prod/demo. In the Manage data modal's Danger zone, restyle the 'Erase all local data' button with a red/danger treatment so the destructive action is visually distinct from the neutral controls. Keep the existing confirm step. NOTE: the source request trailed off mid-sentence ('…and when') — a second requirement was likely intended (possibly a hold-to-confirm, a typed confirmation, or a disabled state). Ship the red-styling part now and flag the missing clause for the owner to clarify before building more. Done when: on staging the Erase button reads as a danger action (red), prod/demo are unchanged, and the open question about the second clause is recorded.

items.title: (staging) Remove the Changelog button from the dashboard top bar
items.prompt: Staging-only — gate to the staging surface; do NOT change prod/demo. Remove the 'Changelog' button from the app dashboard top bar on staging (it links out to the marketing changelog and is noise inside the journal app). Edit the shared top-bar source (partials/app-topbar.html) behind a `<!--IF mode!=staging-->` / mode conditional so prod + demo keep the button until/unless this is promoted via CH16, then rebuild with `node scripts/build-includes.mjs`. Done when: the staging dashboard top bar no longer shows the Changelog button, prod/demo are unchanged, and the build is idempotent.

items.title: (staging) Break-even & Cost Budget always computes from All Time (ignore filters/scope)
items.prompt: Staging-only — gate to the staging surface; do NOT change prod/demo. The Break-even & Cost Budget panel currently recomputes against the active scope toggle + filter bar like the rest of the dashboard. On staging, make it ALWAYS calculate from All Time — independent of scope and filters — so it reads as a stable, account-level cost budget rather than a filtered slice. Source it from the unfiltered trade set (not applyFilters()/SCOPE) inside renderCalc/costModel behind STAGING_PAGE, and update the panel copy to state it's all-time. Leave every other panel filter-driven. Done when: on staging, changing scope/filters does not change the Break-even & Cost Budget figures; prod/demo keep the current filter-aware behavior; no console errors.

items.title: (staging) New module: Trade Blotter (full-width scrollable trades table)
items.prompt: Staging-only — gate to the staging surface; do NOT change prod/demo. Add a new dashboard module, 'Trade Blotter': a full-horizontal-width panel containing a scrollable list of ALL trades — essentially the Manage data → Trades table surfaced on the dashboard. Default dashboard position: directly BELOW the Trading Calendar module (and it must respect drag-reorder/collapse + workspace layout templates). Columns per row: Date/time, Symbol, Instrument name, Contract quantity, Price, Commission, Note, PnL. Rows are READ-ONLY except the Note field, which is inline-editable and persists via the shared annotation/Store path (annTextField / trademeta) — all other editing stays in the Manage data menu. Reuse the existing trade-row rendering + the shared commission helper (commSymRows/costModel) rather than recomputing. Add the markup to partials/app-dash.html behind a `<!--IF mode=staging-->` conditional, render gated by STAGING_PAGE, register its data-key in DEFAULT_DASH_ORDER (in app-dash order), then rebuild with build-includes. Confirm whether the blotter should respect the active filter set/scope like other panels (default: yes). Done when: staging shows a full-width Trade Blotter below the calendar with the eight columns, scrollable, the Note field editable inline and everything else read-only; prod/demo are unchanged; build idempotent and tests green.

items.title: (staging) Performance graph: stack the gross/net/take-home gradient fills
items.prompt: Staging-only — gate to the staging surface; do NOT change prod/demo. On the Performance graph each plot (Gross/Net/Take-home) has a gradient area fill under its line (green/blue/pink), but with multiple plots enabled only the GREEN fill is visible — the topmost fill paints opaquely over the others. On staging, fix the gradient compositing so the enabled fills STACK/blend and each remains legible (e.g. render the fills back-to-front by magnitude, and/or lower the fill opacity so overlaps show through) instead of green sitting solid on top. Keep the line overlays and the hover/click-to-select-date behavior intact. (The source note trailed off at 'when…'.) Done when: on staging, enabling two or three plots shows all their gradients legibly layered, not just green; prod/demo are unchanged; no console errors.

items.title: (staging) Design review: distribute Definitions & Caveats into the relevant panels
items.prompt: Staging-scoped design review. Evaluate REMOVING the standalone 'Definitions & Caveats' dashboard module and DISTRIBUTING its contents into the relevant panels/menus — mirroring how Break-even & Cost Budget now carries its own 'Assumptions & Caveats' subsection (F6/F10). Go through EACH item currently in Definitions & Caveats and recommend which module/menu it should relocate to (e.g. metric definitions → Advanced Statistics or the F14 stat-card detail modals; scope/filter wording → the scope toggle / filter bar; cost/tax model notes → Break-even; date/session/timezone assumptions → wherever they surface). Produce a per-item mapping table and a clear keep-vs-distribute recommendation; weigh discoverability (one place to read everything) vs context (caveats next to the number they qualify). Any implementation is staging-first (proving-ground) until promoted. Done when: a written per-item relocation mapping + recommendation exists, with spun-off implementation items if approved.

items.title: (infra) Cache the per-visitor /api/* calls to extend the Workers free-tier headroom
items.prompt: Cloudflare Pages Functions count against the Workers FREE plan's 100,000 requests/day (combined Workers + Functions). Today every visitor triggers ~1-3 Function calls — `/api/geo` (tax-state prefill on the app landing), `/api/config` (feature flags at app boot), and the homepage `/api/status` Live-pill ping — so the daily cap is the app's real traffic ceiling and a spike could exhaust it (static assets keep serving; the `/api/*` calls start erroring). Reduce these per-visitor invocations: edge-cache `/api/status` and `/api/config` with a short TTL (Cache API / `Cache-Control`) since both change rarely and `/api/config` already mirrors the client-side APP_FLAGS defaults so a briefly-stale read is harmless; cache `/api/geo` by coarse region. Keep admin POST writes uncached. Confirm each call already fails safe when it errors / goes over-limit (geo silently no-ops, config falls back to APP_FLAGS, the pill shows a neutral state). Done when: a typical app session makes materially fewer (ideally cache-served) `/api/*` invocations, all three still work, and each degrades gracefully under the 100k/day cap — with a before/after note on invocations per session.

items.title: (infra guardrail) Rate-data scraper must stay within the 50-subrequest free-tier cap
items.prompt: Guardrail for the roadmap's scheduled rate-data scraper (the Cron Worker that will pull CME fee schedules, per-broker data-feed prices, and per-state tax tables into `data/*.json`). The Workers FREE plan allows only 50 EXTERNAL subrequests per invocation. When this is built, design within that cap: batch/limit outbound fetches to <=50 per run (count the GitHub commit-back call), split sources across multiple scheduled invocations, or move to Workers Paid. Reject a single-invocation design that fans out to dozens of sources on the free tier.

items.title: (infra guardrail) Move to Workers Paid before shipping the paid cloud-sync tier
items.prompt: Guardrail for the planned cloud-sync subscription tier (the `CloudStore` behind the `Store` seam, A4). Every device push/pull is a Pages Function invocation against the Workers FREE plan's 100,000 requests/day cap — a few hundred active sync users would exhaust it — and KV's 1,000 writes/day free cap is far too low for user data. Do NOT ship a paying sync product on the free Workers plan: treat the Workers Paid plan ($5/mo — 10M requests + higher KV/CPU) as a prerequisite, with R2 (10 GB free, free egress — fits the zero-knowledge model) for encrypted trade blobs and D1/KV for accounts/entitlements. Plan the economics around Workers Paid (trivial against subscription revenue), not around stretching the free tier. Reject a sync rollout that assumes the free request/KV caps.

items.title: (infra guardrail) Keep Workers KV low-write — never store per-visitor state in KV
items.prompt: Guardrail for KV usage (`STATUS_KV`). The Workers FREE plan caps KV at 1,000 WRITES/day (plus 1,000 deletes + 1,000 lists; reads are 100k/day; 1 GB storage). Today KV holds only admin-set status + feature-flag config and the best-effort admin rate-limiter — all low-write and fail-open, which is fine. Keep it that way: never put per-VISITOR or per-REQUEST state in KV (counters, analytics, sessions, public-endpoint rate-limiting) — that would blow 1,000 writes/day almost immediately and start erroring. New per-user/per-request persistence belongs in D1/R2 (and on Workers Paid). Reject a change that writes to KV on a public or high-traffic path.

items.title: (infra guardrail) Repo layout is the deploy contract — no /src+bundler reorg
items.prompt: Guardrail from R16's structure review. Because the project has NO build step, the repo root IS the Cloudflare Pages deploy/web root — the folder layout is simultaneously the public URL structure AND the deploy contract. Do NOT adopt a '/src vs /public with a bundler' reorganization: it either changes every public URL (breaking canonicals/sitemap/_redirects/bookmarks) or requires a build/copy step that violates the 'no build step / deploys as static files' design pillar. Keep root = web root (functions/ MUST stay at root for Pages Functions; _headers/_redirects/robots.txt/sitemap.xml/favicon stay at root). IF a reorg is ever truly needed, the ONLY Pages-compatible move is a public/ output-dir split (browser-served files under public/, Pages 'build output directory' = public, functions/+scripts/+partials/+tooling stay at root) — and it must update the FULL coupled set in lockstep: the absolute /app/ /assets/ /data/ URL references, _redirects, _headers, robots.txt, sitemap.xml + page canonicals (CH5/CH6), build-includes.mjs (the ['.', 'app'] HTML scan), build-manifest.mjs (data/* paths), and bump-version.mjs (the prod-shipping path prefixes). Reject piecemeal moves that update only some of these.

items.title: Guardrail: admin-endpoint security must not depend on the fail-open rate limiter
items.prompt: From the R1 audit. rateLimited() (functions/_lib/http.js ~19-30) returns false (ALLOW) whenever STATUS_KV is unbound and is best-effort/non-atomic; it is the only throttle on /api/status, /api/config, and /api/admin-key (token issuance, 20/60s). This is the correct trade-off (don't lock the admin out; endpoints sit behind Access + constant-time token/key compare), so it is defense-in-depth only. Guardrail: never let an endpoint's security DEPEND on rateLimited() — auth (Access JWT + constant-time compare) is the real control; reject any future endpoint that relies on the limiter as primary protection, and keep STATUS_KV bound in prod so the throttle is actually active. Adjacent: A17 (KV low-write), S11 (webhook signature).

items.title: (guardrail) Dev tooling stays dev-only — SUPERSEDED IN PART by ADR-001/A28
items.prompt: PARTIALLY SUPERSEDED by docs/adr-001-vite-svelte-spa.md. This guardrail was written for the R19 Tier A world where the shipped app was zero-dep and the committed files were the deploy artifact (so devDeps could never touch shipped bytes, and SKIP_DEPENDENCY_INSTALL=1 was set on Pages). ADR-001 reverses that: Tier B (Vite build) is being adopted, so build deps now DO produce shipped bytes and Pages WILL install + build. The replaced clauses: 'shipped output stays dependency-free', the drift gate as proof-of-no-shipped-deps, and SKIP_DEPENDENCY_INSTALL — those no longer hold after A26 lands. WHAT STILL APPLIES until/after migration: keep node_modules + build output (playwright-report, test-results, the Vite dist/output dir) gitignored; pin every dep with a committed lockfile. The NEW dependency discipline lives in A28 (minimal/pinned/audited; supply chain as a security control; local-compute pillar gates every dep). Reference A28, not this item, going forward.

items.title: (guardrail) Dependency policy — minimal/pinned/audited; supply chain is a security control
items.prompt: Guardrail from ADR-001 (docs/adr-001-vite-svelte-spa.md), replacing the zero-runtime-dep stance (A25). Runtime/build dependencies are now ALLOWED, under discipline — because for a privacy product handling financial data, the supply chain IS a security control. (1) EARN-ITS-WEIGHT: add a dep only when hand-rolling it is materially worse; keep writing small utilities by hand (assets/util.js). Heavyweight enablers qualify (the framework, charting like lightweight-charts, layout/virtualization libs); date/utility micro-libs do not. (2) SUPPLY CHAIN: pin every version + commit the lockfile; run `npm audit` in CI; apply Subresource Integrity / strict CSP to shipped bundles; keep the dependency surface minimal; consider vendoring the few critical libs so a registry compromise can't silently reach users. (3) LOCAL-COMPUTE PILLAR GATES EVERY DEP (hard): reject any dep or dep feature that phones home, ships telemetry/analytics, or could exfiltrate trade data — no exceptions, regardless of convenience. Reject any dependency addition that fails the earn-its-weight bar or the local-compute gate.

items.title: (guardrail) Preserve the pure-logic core verbatim through the framework migration
items.prompt: Guardrail from ADR-001 (docs/adr-001-vite-svelte-spa.md). The Svelte migration (A27 + later Phase 4) must touch the VIEW LAYER ONLY. The pure-logic modules are the hard-won, test-covered value and must be imported into components VERBATIM, not rewritten: app/adapters.js (platform CSV adapters + detection + pairFills), compute() (metrics), costModel() (commissions/subscriptions/tax/take-home), the Section-1256 tax model, app/store.js (the Store seam — A4), and assets/util.js. They stay framework-agnostic so the node test suites (test-adapters/test-version/test-auth/test-flags/test-tax) keep covering them unchanged. Reject a framework PR that reimplements adapter parsing, the metrics/cost/tax math, or persistence inside a component instead of importing the existing module. The Store interface (A4) remains the only persistence seam.

items.title: Source-tree reorg (src/ + static split) — gated on the Phase 4 Svelte migration
items.prompt: DEFERRED — gated on ADR-001 Phase 4 (prod + demo Svelte migration), step 4d. The Vite build (A26) DECOUPLED the source layout from the public URL structure (Vite maps any source path to any output URL), so the A18 ban on a /src reorg NO LONGER APPLIES — a source-tree reorganization is now an optional cleanup, not forbidden. BENEFITS: (1) separate served SOURCE from non-served tooling (functions/scripts/partials/docs/configs at root today are mixed with site source); (2) resolve the mixed assets/ dir (bundled JS modules like home.js/util.js vs verbatim-static SVG/PNG) by splitting bundled vs a single static/public dir — which ALSO lets us adopt Vite's native publicDir and DELETE scripts/copy-static.mjs; (3) a Svelte-shaped structure (components/lib/routes). COST: it IS the A18 lockstep migration — moving source files changes the path PREFIXES that build-includes/build-manifest/bump-version/eslint/jsconfig/.prettierignore/README image refs key off, plus every relative import (./core.js, ../assets/util.js) — so it must be done ALL-AT-ONCE with all coupled paths + imports updated together (URLs stay 1:1 regardless, per A26). WHY PHASE 4, NOT NOW: doing it during the in-flight A27 buildout creates huge rename diffs that bury the real work; the final structure depends on the Svelte shape (don't reorg twice); and the flat layout's last advantage — the root tree still being directly servable un-built — is already eroding (staging.html can't be served un-built now) and disappears entirely once prod/demo are Svelte, so the cost/benefit only improves up to Phase 4. Settle the structure ONCE, after A33. Done when: either the reorg lands with Phase 4 (target layout decided; all coupled paths + imports updated in lockstep; URLs still 1:1; copy-static.mjs retired in favor of publicDir; all surfaces verified) OR it is explicitly re-affirmed as deferred.

items.title: Discuss: migrate from JS to TypeScript
items.prompt: DISCUSSION/ANALYSIS (no migration yet). Evaluate moving from today's JSDoc-typed JS (tsc --checkJs, opt-in // @ts-check — CH33) to native TypeScript, now that a Vite build exists (ADR-001). Cover: what TS buys beyond the current JSDoc setup (the pure-logic core already type-checks); migration path/sequence (.js->.ts incrementally, <script lang="ts"> in .svelte, tsconfig/jsconfig + build changes, keeping the node test suites running); risks against the guardrails (A29 verbatim pure-logic core; A28 dependency/supply-chain policy); and a recommendation with a phased plan. Done when: a written pros/cons + recommendation is delivered and any follow-up migration items are filed.

items.title: Discuss: adopt SvelteKit (pros/cons + analysis)
items.prompt: DISCUSSION/ANALYSIS (no migration). Analyze adopting SvelteKit vs. the current Vite multi-page + Svelte 5 SPA (ADR-001). Cover: what SvelteKit adds (file-routing, SSR/SSG, endpoints, adapters) vs. what we actually need; the HARD local-compute pillar (no trade data leaves the browser — any SSR must never see trade data); fit with Cloudflare Pages/Functions (adapter-cloudflare) and the existing functions/ edge layer; impact on the deploy contract (A18/A26); and whether the static marketing pages would benefit from SSG. End with a recommendation + phased plan or an explicit defer. Done when: a written pros/cons + recommendation is delivered and follow-ups filed.

items.title: Review PR history + backfill the changelog
items.prompt: Review the merged PR history on main against data/changelog.json (curated, prod-version-keyed user notes — F13) and backfill missing entries. Known gap: the changelog jumps 0.20.5 -> 0.25.0, so the 0.21-0.24 prod bumps (PRs #37-#39: Svelte staging parity A31/A32, A34-A41, A42-A54) have no notes. For each prod bump with user-visible change, add a concise user-facing entry keyed by its prod version, matching the existing voice (this is for users, not a git log). Bumps with no user-facing effect may be skipped but should be noted. Do not hand-edit data/versions.json. Done when: every prod version with user-facing change has a changelog entry and the gap is closed or explained.

items.title: Discuss: which Vite features to utilize
items.prompt: DISCUSSION/ANALYSIS. Survey Vite capabilities we don't yet use that would pay off now that the build exists (ADR-001/A26): code-splitting / dynamic import for the heavier roadmap modules (R11/R12/R13/F23), asset & image handling, env/define, dependency pre-bundling, bundle-analysis + size budgets, legacy/target tuning, and CSP/SRI-friendly output (ties to S18/A55). For each: benefit vs. cost and fit with the constraints (A28 deps, local-compute). Recommendation + any follow-up items. Done when: a written shortlist with recommendations is delivered.

items.title: Discuss: which Svelte features to utilize
items.prompt: DISCUSSION/ANALYSIS. Survey Svelte 5 features we under-use and where they'd help in the runes-first SPA: $derived/$effect patterns, snippets, stores vs. context (we use context('bb:store')), transitions/animations, actions (we have styleProps/modal), lazy/async components, error boundaries, and the compiler a11y warnings. Map each to current/roadmap UI (curve, calendar, the module menu R12, resizable modules R13, blotter F23). Benefit vs. complexity; recommendation + follow-ups. Done when: a written shortlist with recommendations is delivered.

items.title: CRITICAL: exhaustive audit of all data calculations
items.prompt: P1 - CRITICAL. Exhaustive correctness audit of EVERY calculation Blotterbook performs on imported data - this is the platform's core value and must be air-tight. Scope (pure-logic core, A29): app/adapters.js (CSV parse, fills pairing via pairFills(), normalization, symbol/root, side, qty, holdMs, date/timezone handling); app/core.js compute() (PnL, win rate, profit factor, expectancy, equity curve + drawdown, streaks, day/session bucketing, averages); app/core.js costModel() (per-broker commission tiers, exchange/clearing/NFA fees, micros, data-feed + platform subscriptions, Section-1256 tax via state-tax.json, blended rate, take-home); app/curveseries.js (gross/net/take series); app/report.js. Verify against hand-worked examples and edge cases: empty/partial fills, same-symbol reversals, multi-contract, fractional/large qty, missing fields, DST/year boundaries (note isoWeek tweak A58), float-accumulation rounding, negative/zero PnL, micros vs minis, per-state tax. Confirm the node fixtures (test-adapters/test-tax) are sufficient and add cases for gaps. READ-ONLY audit: file a fix item for each discrepancy, don't fix inline. Done when: a thorough, prioritized written audit covering every calc path is delivered with fix items filed.

items.title: Review the CI configuration
items.prompt: Review .github/workflows/ (ci.yml, version-bump.yml) for correctness, coverage, and hygiene now that the build is Vite + Svelte (ADR-001). Check: node version matches .node-version (22); npm ci + caching; the full gate runs and fails closed (lint, typecheck, format, unit, build, e2e, and the include/manifest drift gate); version-bump triggers/permissions are correct (CH12) and don't self-retrigger ([skip ci]); Playwright uses the preinstalled Chromium rather than re-downloading; secrets handling; and any redundant or missing steps. Done when: a written review with concrete fixes (and any low-risk fixes applied) is delivered.

items.title: Audit the documentation for currency + accuracy
items.prompt: Audit all project docs for currency and accuracy against the shipped code (post ADR-001 / A33 Svelte cutover / A55 CSP): README.md, CLAUDE.md, docs/architecture.md, docs/adr-001-vite-svelte-spa.md, docs/build-step-decision.md, functions/README.md, and in-file header comments. Flag anything describing the deleted vanilla layer as current, stale commands/paths, incorrect file maps, or claims that no longer hold (build step, CSP, surfaces). Verify cross-links/anchors resolve. Fix inaccuracies (docs-only). Done when: no doc misstates the current architecture and the file maps/commands are correct.

items.title: Convert the homepage, admin, and info site to Svelte
items.prompt: Evaluate, then (if approved) convert the remaining static surfaces - homepage (index.html + home.css), admin (admin.html + admin.css + assets/admin.js), and the info site (howto/roadmap/changelog/legal + site.css, partials/nav+footer, assets/changelog.js) - to Svelte under the Vite build, unifying the shared chrome (nav/footer) and the changelog/admin rendering with the app's component model. Constraints: marketing pages MUST stay fast + SEO-friendly (prerendered/static output, NOT pulled behind the SPA shell - see ADR-001); URLs preserved 1:1 (deploy contract A18/A26); CSP stays style-src 'self' (A55); tokens.css remains the single source. Sequence after the SvelteKit decision (A62) and alongside the structure reorg (A30/A70). Done when: a plan (and, if greenlit, the conversion) ships with every page rendering identically, URLs + SEO intact, and drift/e2e green.

items.title: Audit project structure to optimize for Vite + Svelte
items.prompt: Audit the repo structure now that Vite + Svelte are in place (ADR-001) and recommend optimizations. Cover the deferred A30 source-tree reorg (separate served source from tooling; split assets/ into bundled-JS vs verbatim-static; retire copy-static.mjs for Vite publicDir; a components/lib/ layout for app/staging-svelte/ incl. the A30 dir rename), where the pure-logic core should live, whether the root-flat deploy contract (A18) still earns its keep, and any dead/duplicated files. Must preserve URLs 1:1 and the build/drift gates. READ-ONLY audit + prioritized, sequenced recommendations; file follow-up items. Done when: a written structure audit with a concrete plan is delivered.

items.title: (staging) Top module headers clickable with a menu popup
items.prompt: (staging) The module/panel headers along the top should be clickable to open a menu popup (per-module actions and/or add/spawn). Reconcile with R12 (module menu - spawn modules onto the dashboard) so the two don't diverge. Build in the Svelte app (app/staging-svelte/), gate to the right surface, keep demo non-mutating, and stay CSP-clean (no inline styles - use the styleProps action / utility classes per A55). Done when: clicking a top module opens an accessible menu popup with working actions, e2e covers open/close, and the scope is reconciled with R12.

items.title: (staging) Graph gets an unwanted highlight on calendar click
items.prompt: (staging) BUG: clicking a calendar day draws an unwanted blue focus/selection highlight around the performance graph. The graph should not be highlighted when the interaction target is the calendar. Trace where the curve container gains focus / aria-selected / outline on day-select (EquityCurve.svelte + the calendar->select wiring in App.svelte) and scope it so calendar clicks update the selected day WITHOUT visually highlighting the graph, while keeping real keyboard-focus styling on the graph intact (a11y). Done when: clicking calendar days never draws the graph highlight, genuine keyboard focus still shows, and an e2e/visual check confirms.

items.title: (staging) Paginate the Manage-data trades table
items.prompt: (staging) Optimize the Manage data panel: the loaded-trades table renders the entire dataset at once. Add paging (page through chunks, or windowed/virtualized rendering) so large imports don't render thousands of rows at once, while keeping search + filter working across the FULL dataset, not just the visible page. Pure presentation over the already-loaded data (ManageData.svelte); keep demo non-mutating. Consider sharing the windowing approach with the F23 Trade Blotter. Done when: the table loads in pages/windows, search+filter still span all trades, large imports stay responsive, and an e2e covers paging + filtered search.

items.title: (staging) Export-report modal broken on mobile
items.prompt: (staging) Debug the Export report modal/preview on mobile (~360-414px): the report iframe/preview and the format / Download / Email controls likely overflow or render poorly on narrow screens. Make the modal, the controls bar, and the report preview usable on mobile (responsive layout, no horizontal PAGE scroll - consistent with A51) without changing the report content. Verify the PDF / PNG / JPEG / Markdown / email paths still work. Done when: the export-report modal is usable at 360px and 414px with no page-level horizontal scroll, controls wrap cleanly, and all export formats still function.

howToDrive: One item per prompt; one surface per prompt where possible.
Lead with the *goal*, not the mechanism; let me pick the workable path.
If you batch, name the 1–3 must-haves in case I run low on budget.
Keep using the change-plan + memory pattern for any multi-step item.
